errors.generic: Algo salió mal. Por favor, inténtelo de nuevo.
errors.network: Error de conexión de red. Compruebe su conexión.
errors.unauthorized: Sesión expirada. Por favor, inicie sesión de nuevo.

contractErrors.generic: Falló la ejecución del contrato. Por favor, inténtelo de nuevo.
contractErrors.limitExceeded: Límite de operación del contrato excedido.

pending.label: Confirmando
pending.emphasis: Esperando la confirmación de la red

unknown.label: Estado desconocido
unknown.emphasis: Aún sin confirmar — revise el explorador

dashboard.loadError: No pudimos cargar el resumen de tu panel.

bills.pageSubtitle: [ES] Manage and track your recurring bills
bills.billCreationDesc: [ES] The initiating form should own validation and contract-build feedback. Longer-running submit states should move into a stack that stays visible while the user continues working.
bills.billWarning: [ES] This bill request is built as an on-chain USDC payment payload. Your wallet signs and submits the transaction; RemitWise only prepares the payload.

form.repeatDescOff: [ES] Leave off for one-time bills, or turn on to add a visible payment schedule.
form.repeatDescOn: [ES] {{preview}}; reminders start {{days}} days before each due date.

status.idleDesc: [ES] Validation lives at field level, contract-build feedback stays inline above the CTA, and submit progress should move into the persistent stack rail.
status.pendingDesc: [ES] Hold the user in this form context until the bill payload is ready for wallet approval.
status.successDesc: [ES] The next step should open wallet approval immediately, while a stacked confirmation card remains visible if the user navigates away.
status.errorTitle: [ES] Bill request could not be prepared

asyncPanel.title: [ES] Bill Submission Pattern
asyncPanel.desc: [ES] Recurring bill creation is a good example of why inline build states and persistent submission stacks should be separate surfaces.
asyncPanel.queueDesc: [ES] On desktop, anchor stacked confirmation cards near the top-right edge of the main content. On mobile, move the same stack directly below the initiating form or modal footer.
asyncPanel.footer: [ES] This pass does not require any Tailwind config changes. It uses existing red focus rings, dark surfaces, and border-opacity utilities already present across the app.

billStages.stage1Detail: [ES] Keep field errors attached to the triggering inputs so users do not need to reconcile a toast with the form.
billStages.stage2Detail: [ES] The form card should own the build state because the user still needs the bill amount, schedule, and recurring toggle in view.
billStages.stage3Detail: [ES] Open a focused confirmation step only after the contract payload succeeds and the user can act immediately.
billStages.stage4Detail: [ES] Show network progress in a stacked surface that persists even after the form scrolls away.

billQueue.item1Detail: [ES] Primary submission remains expanded until wallet approval or an error resolves.
billQueue.item2Detail: [ES] Secondary tasks compress so multiple actions never dominate the screen.
billQueue.item3Detail: [ES] Leave the success state visible briefly so the user can trust the outcome without opening history.
